settings.title: How to Spot Pain in a Pet That Hides It
settings.excerpt: Animals instinctively mask discomfort, so the early signs are usually changes in habit rather than obvious limping.
settings.body:
One of the hardest parts of caring for an animal is that they cannot tell you when something hurts. By the time a pet is visibly limping, the problem is often well advanced.

The earliest clues tend to be small changes in routine. Watch for:

  • Hesitating before jumping onto a favourite spot.
  • Sleeping more, or in different places than usual.
  • A change in appetite, grooming, or temper.

None of these mean panic, but together they are worth a conversation. If your pet is just not themselves, trust that instinct and give Tailside a call. We would always rather check early.
